Discovery: World's largest sportswear brand at a 42% drawdown to $46 (52-week low was $42.09 on April 9) with new CEO Elliott Hill's 'Win Now' turnaround showing first green shoots — wholesale revenue up 5-8% as channel rebuild takes hold — and Q4 FY2026 earnings in late June will serve as the first full-year turnaround scorecard.
value_picks.analyst_take: Cook+Hill coordinated $1.92M cluster buy at 52W lows; 41.6% off high; new CEO Hill 'Win Now' showing first green shoots (wholesale +5-8%); Q4 FY2026 earnings June 25 as turnaround scorecard

Discovery: The 2026 Iran war created a real-world proving ground for affordable unmanned and hypersonic systems — exactly Kratos's niche; Kratos leads MACH-TB 2.0 Task Area 1 hypersonic test program (targeting ~20 Rocket Lab launches), its unmanned segment grew 35.8% YoY in Q3 2025, and the hypersonic segment is projected to double revenues in 2026; record $1.6B backlog and $13.7B pipeline with 18-analyst Strong Buy consensus and $96.71 average price target (~30% upside from current levels).
value_picks.analyst_take: 47.6% off 52W high; MACH-TB 2.0 $1.45B hypersonic contract; unmanned segment +35.8% YoY; $1.6B backlog; $13.7B pipeline; 18-analyst Strong Buy $115 consensus — Iran war proving ground for hypersonics

Discovery: Down 44% in a single month from $386.78 52-week high to ~$217 — same-store sales declining for 5+ consecutive quarters triggered indiscriminate selling, but multiple analyst upgrades (Raymond James Strong Buy $240, Citi Buy $230) at current levels call the reaction overdone, management announced bold share buyback as confidence signal, and Q1 2026 earnings on April 29 is the critical binary catalyst — any SSS improvement would confirm the H2 2026 recovery narrative and likely trigger a sharp squeeze; risk is Q1 SSS disappoints again (-5.8% in Q4), validating bear thesis and pushing stock to ~$175.
value_picks.analyst_take: 48% off 52W high; Q1 2026 earnings April 29 is binary — any SSS improvement confirms H2 recovery; Raymond James Strong Buy $240, Citi Buy $230; management buyback as conviction signal; 13.1% SI

Discovery: Iran war and ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict are accelerating Western utilities' flight from Russian uranium enrichment services, while aggressive Chinese and Indian purchasing is shrinking the pool of Western-aligned supply; Cameco — now an integrated fuel provider through its 49% Westinghouse stake — is the premier safe-haven uranium play, with analysts bullish on multi-year contract repricing as utilities rush to lock in non-Russian supply chains before spot prices rise further.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Strong uranium bull thesis (Russia/Iran driving Western utility supply diversification) but 65x forward PE and 10.1% off 52W high limits margin of safety; wait for $105-115 entry
